reddit_title: Got hit by a semi truck in El Paso, TX — what steps should I take to protect my case and rights?
reddit_body:
Semi truck accidents in El Paso are unfortunately common given the heavy freight traffic on I-10, US-54, and routes near the Bridge of the Americas. If you were recently involved in one, the most important thing to understand is that these cases move fast — and not always in your favor if you wait.

First, preserve everything: photos, dashcam footage, witness contacts, and the police report number. Trucking companies are required to maintain driver logs, inspection records, and black box (ECM) data under FMCSA rules, but they can legally purge some of that data quickly. Sending a spoliation letter through an attorney early is critical. Also avoid giving recorded statements to the carrier's insurance company before you have legal advice.

For finding representation, a service like wreckmatch.com can match you with attorneys who specifically handle commercial truck accident cases in Texas — which is a very different animal from a standard car accident claim. Liability can extend to the driver, the carrier, the cargo loader, and even the truck manufacturer depending on the facts. Getting the right attorney early makes a real difference.
